tools.name: notes_read
tools.description: Read the contents of a note.

path.description: Note path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

tools.name: notes_read_lines
tools.description: Read a range of lines from a note with line numbers. Returns lines in the format {line_number}|{content}. Use this instead of notes_read when you only need a specific portion of a note to reduce context usage.

path.description: Note path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

start_line.description: Line number to start reading at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). Defaults to start_line (single line read) if omitted.

tools.name: notes_list
tools.description: List files and directories at a given path. When an AGENTS.md file exists in the listed directory, its contents are automatically appended to the listing as a context section — this is not part of the ls output but an automatic context-loading feature.

path.description: Directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

long.description: Show detailed format with permissions, size, and dates

tools.name: notes_search
tools.description: Search note contents for a pattern using grep. Returns matching lines with context.

path.description: Note or directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

recursive.description: Search directories recursively. On by default — set to false only when searching a single note path.

files_only.description: Show only file names that contain matches, not the matching lines

tools.name: notes_write
tools.description: Write content to a note. Creates the note if it does not exist, overwrites if it does. Parent directories are created automatically if they do not exist.

path.description: Note path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

content.description: Full note content in markdown format including YAML frontmatter

tools.name: notes_write_lines
tools.description: Replace a range of lines in a note without rewriting the entire note. Lines outside the range are preserved unchanged. Returns a diff showing removed lines (-prefix), added lines (+prefix), and surrounding context so you can verify the edit. Use this instead of notes_write for targeted edits to large notes.

path.description: Note path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

start_line.description: Line number to start replacing at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end replacing at (1-indexed, inclusive). Defaults to start_line (single line replacement) if omitted.

original_content.description: The content currently at [start_line, end_line]. The tool verifies this matches the note before applying the patch. If the only difference is trailing whitespace, the note's original trailing whitespace is preserved in the replacement. If it doesn't match, the edit is rejected with the expected vs actual content — re-read the note and retry.

content.description: Replacement content for the specified line range

tools.name: notes_replace
tools.description: Replace all occurrences of a pattern in a note. Supports capture groups ($1-$9) in the replacement string. Returns a diff of changes made. The pattern is matched against the full note content with multiline mode enabled (^ and $ match line boundaries). When the regex pattern matches 0 times, the tool automatically retries with a trailing-whitespace-tolerant literal search that ignores trailing whitespace differences between the pattern and the note content (auto-retry matches must align with line boundaries). When the regex pattern matches an unreasonable number of positions (likely due to unintentional regex metacharacters like | in a markdown table), the tool automatically retries as literal mode. Use literal mode explicitly when you want to ensure the pattern is matched as-is without regex interpretation. Trailing whitespace on matched lines is preserved from the original when using literal mode; in regex mode, trailing whitespace within the match span is replaced, while trailing whitespace outside the match span is preserved.

path.description: Note path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

pattern.description: Search pattern. By default, interpreted as regex matched against the full note content with multiline mode (^ and $ match line boundaries). In literal mode, the pattern is treated as literal text — no regex interpretation. For multi-line patterns, include actual newlines in the string.

replacement.description: Replacement string. Supports $1-$9 for capture group references (regex mode with capture groups only — when the pattern has no capture groups, $1-$9 are treated as literal text). Use $$ for a literal $ when the pattern has capture groups. Use an empty string to delete matches. For multi-line replacements, include actual newlines in the string. Note: if the regex pattern unintentionally contains capture groups (e.g., parentheses in code), $1-$9 in the replacement will expand those unintended groups — use literal: true or escape parentheses with \(\) to avoid this.

max_replacements.description: Maximum number of replacements to apply. 0 (default) means unlimited. Use 1 to replace only the first match and avoid unintended changes when the same pattern appears in multiple locations.

literal.description: Treat the pattern as literal text instead of regex. In literal mode, the pattern can match as a substring within a line (standard find-and-replace semantics). The tool auto-detects degenerate regex matches (e.g., unintentional alternation from | in markdown tables) and retries as literal with line-boundary enforcement, but setting this explicitly ensures the pattern is never interpreted as regex and allows mid-line substring matches. Capture groups ($1-$9) are not supported in literal mode. Default: false.

line_numbers.description: Show line numbers in the returned diff (default: true)

tools.name: notes_delete
tools.description: Delete a note. Refuses to delete directories.

path.description: Note path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

tools.name: notes_delete_dir
tools.description: Delete an empty directory. Refuses to delete non-empty directories or files.

path.description: Directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)
